flags.concern: Final-video duration handling: Recurring debt: step 4 still swaps in a new final-video asset without any duration source, so a shorter regenerated final video can be asked to play for the old group span and overrun the real media length.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:23-37` still fetches only `id, location, thumbnail_url, shot_id, created_at` with no duration metadata. The existing final-video insertion path in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:137-153` also creates final-video assets with only `file/type/generationId` and sizes the clip from the timeline span. Playback then trims by `clip.to` in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:115-118`, so the proposed update-to-latest path inherits the same shorter-media overrun risk unless the plan adds a duration source or explicit containment.

flags.concern: Final-video refresh path: the plan assumes `useShotFinalVideos()` refreshes every 30s, but in this repo it does not poll; stale detection only refreshes when some other invalidation fires. The badge/update flow is under-specified unless the implementation explicitly relies on the existing invalidation path or adds refetching.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:20-37` sets `staleTime: 30_000` but has no `refetchInterval`, so React Query will not auto-refresh every 30s. The actual refresh hook-up is the broader completed-task invalidation in `src/shared/hooks/useRealtimeInvalidation.ts:109-121`, which is different from the plan's stated polling assumption and manual verification step.
